title: Echinopsis bonnieae
taxonomy: Echinopsis bonnieae is classified within the family Cactaceae. This classification is due to its characteristic cactus morphology. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. Further classification places it within the tribe Cacteae. Echinopsis bonnieae is a species of flowering plant.

intro: Echinopsis bonnieae, commonly known as the 'Bonnie' or 'Bonnie Pachanoi', is a species of cactus native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is renowned for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Echinopsis bonnieae include alkaloids such as bonnieine, which is thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are believed to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
